channel.title: Rice on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Rice on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Rice Greek Salad
item.description:
Submitted by Lynn Sayer

Cousin of the Bride

  • 2 cups cooked and cooled, Uncle Ben’s Converted Brand rice
  • 3/4 cup English cucumber, cut into slices
  • 3/4 cup tomatoes, diced
  • 3/4 cup green and red peppers, diced
  • 1/3 cup red onion, diced
  • 1 cup Feta cheese, cut into large dice
  • 1/3 cup Kalamata olives, pitted

Dressing:

Ingredients

  • 1/3 cup olive oil
  • 1/4 cup red wine vinegar
  • 1 garlic, minced (about 1 tsp)
  • 1 tsp rubbed oregano
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/2 tsp pepper

Instructions

In a large bowl, combine cooked rice with all diced vegetables, diced cheese and olives.

item.title: "Cook Up" Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Naz and Brian Lloyd

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 3 ½ lbs of skinless chicken (cut up 2 whole chickens)
  • 1 ½ cup rice
  • 1-540 mL can black-eyed peas
  • 1-400 mL can coconut milk
  • 3 tbsp butter
  • 3 tbsp soya sauce
  • 2 tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 tsp basil
  • 3 cloves of garlic, minced
  • 1 large onion, minced
  • pinch of black pepper
  • salt to taste

Instructions

Wash chicken and cut into pieces (leave bones in). Place chicken in a large bowl. Blend in onion, garlic, soya sauce, basil, pepper and salt.

item.title: Sausage and Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Lori Girard

Friend of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 can of tomatoes, or stewed tomatoes
  • one onion, cut in long slivers
  • 1 pkg beef bouillon
  • ¾ cup water
  • basil, garlic, salt and pepper

Instructions

Fry onion. Add other ingredients. Thicken sauce with corn starch. Fry breakfast sausage. Slice sausage and add to sauce.

Pour sauce with sausage over white rice.

item.title: Baked Rice
item.description:
Submitted by Linda Desrochers

Cousin of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 2 cups of Uncle Ben’s converted rice
  • 4 cups water
  • ½ cup soya sauce
  • ½ cup oil
  • 1 pkg onion soup mix
  • 1 can drained mushrooms
  • 1 can drained peas

Instructions

Mix all ingredients in roast pan. Place in oven for one hour at 350 degrees.

item.title: Campbell's Citrus Skillet Chicken & Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 4 boneless, skinless chicken breast halves,(about 1 lb/500 gr.)
  • 1-1/4 cups Campbell’s Ready to UseBroth or 1 can Condensed ChickenBroth.
  • 1 medium onion, chopped
  • ¾ cup orange juice
  • 1 cup uncooked regular long grain rice
  • 1-1/2 cups fresh (cut-up) or frozen greenbeans
  • 1 tsp grated orange peel
  • 3 Tbsp chopped fresh parsley or l Tbsp dried parsley flakes

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high in large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned on both sides. Set chicken aside.

item.title: Campbell's Chicken & Vegetable Rice Skillet
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Tbsp vegetable oil
  • 1 lb Boneless, skinless chicken breasthalves, cut in cubes
  • 1 can Cream of Chicken Soup
  • 1 cup water
  • 1 pkg (6 oz/165 g) seasoned long-grainrice mix
  • 2 cups thinly sliced carrots
  • 2 green onions, sliced

Instructions

Heat oil at medium-high heat in large skillet. Add chicken and cook until browned on all sides. Reduce heat to medium.

Stir in mixture of soup, water, rice mix and carrots. Heat to a boil. Reduce heat to low.

item.title: Spanish Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup rice
  • 4 tbsp butter
  • 3 – 4 onions, chopped
  • 1 28-oz can tomatoes
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 1 red or green pepper, chopped
  • 1 tsp paprika
  • 2 tsp salt

Instructions

Saute onions, garlic and pepper in butter. Add remaining ingredients and cook slowly on top of stove for 1 hour.

YIELD: Four servings.

item.title: Ruth's Beef and Rice Supreme
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb hamburg
  • 1 cup rice
  • ½ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp pepper
  • 1 tin tomato soup
  • 1 tin water
  • 3 onions

Instructions

Mix together uncooked hamburg, rice, salt, pepper, soup and water. Slice onions on top. Bake in a covered casserole in a 350 degree oven for 2 hours.

item.title: Oven Fried Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups uncooked long grain rice
  • 1 tbsp soya sauce
  • 2 10-oz cans consommé soup
  • ½ cup oil
  • 2 10-oz cans mushrooms & liquid
  • Celery, onions and green pepper – to taste, chopped fine
  • 1 envelope onion soup mix

Instructions

Mix all ingredients in small roaster and bake in 350 degree oven for 1-1/2 hours.

item.title: Chuk's Chicken Fried Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • 2 green peppers
  • 2 onions
  • 2 cups celery
  • mushroom pieces and stems
  • 1/2 cup soya sauce
  • 2 tins consommé soup
  • 4 cups minute rice

Instructions

Saute diced green peppers, chopped onions, chopped celery, mushroom in butter til onions are

transparent. Add soya sauce and soup. Bring this mixture to a boil and stir in minute rice.

Cover and cook on low heat for 1/2 hour.

Cooked chicken chunks, or ham, or egg can be added when rice is added.

item.title: Wild Rice Many-Grain Bread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup wild rice
  • 2 Tbsp granulated sugar
  • ½ cup warm water
  • 1 pkg dry yeast (or 1 Tbsp )
  • 5 cups (approx.) all-purpose flour
  • ¼ cup fancy molasses
  • 2 Tbsp shortening
  • 1 tsp salt
  • ½ cup natural bran
  • ½ cup raw sunflower seeds
  • ¾ cup 7-grain cereal
  • ¾ cup rolled oats

Instructions

  1.  In saucepan, cook wild rice in 2 cups water (500 ml) water for about 45 minutes or until tender; drain and let cool.

  2.  Meanwhile, dissolve 1 tsp (5 ml) of the sugar in warm water. Sprinkle in yeast; let soften, stirring together until dissolved. Stir in ¼ cup (50 ml) of the flour to make a thin paste; set in warm place for 10 minutes or until bubbly.

  3.  In large bowl and using electric mixer, combine molasses, remaining sugar, shortening, salt and 2 cups (500 ml) warm water. In order, beat in bran, sunflower seeds, yeast mixture, wild rice, 7-grain cereal and rolled oats. With wooden spoon, beat in as much of the remaining flour as necessary to make firm dough. Turn out onto lightly floured surface; knead for about 10 minutes or until smooth and elastic, kneading in as much of the remaining flour as necessary..

  4.  Place in large greased bowl, turning to grease all over. Cover with plastic wrap; let rice in warm draft-free place until doubled in bulk, about 2 hours. (NOTE: I tried the plastic wrap and found the dough stuck to it even if it was lightly greased; I choose instead to make sure that there is flour on the surface and then cover it with a clean cloth.)

  5.  Punch down dough; turn out onto lightly floured surface. Divide into thirds. With rolling pin, flatten each to about 7-1/2-inch (19 cm) square; firmly roll up each into log. Place each in greased 8- x 4-inch pans. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 1-1/2 hours.

  6.  Bake in center of 375 F (190 C) oven for 15 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped on bottom. Remove from pans; let cool on racks. Makes 3 loaves, 12 slices each.

item.title: Balsamic "Caprese" Rice Bowl
item.description:
Ingredients

Phase 1: For the Steamer

  • 2 cups Long-grain rice (Basmati or Jasmine, rinsed)
  • 3 cups Broth (Chicken or Vegetable) or water
  • 6 cloves Garlic, smashed
  • 2 tbsp Butter or Olive oil
  • 2 pinches of Sea salt

Phase 2: The Fresh Components

  • 3 Roma tomatoes, diced
  • 1 cup Fresh basil, sliced into ribbons
  • 1 cup Shredded cheese (Mozzarella or Italian blend)
  • 4 tbsp Balsamic vinegar (or 1 tbsp Balsamic Glaze)

Instructions

item.title: Low Fat Sweet and Sour Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

Note:  Make the sauce below for 1 cup of pre-cooked rice.  Just double or triple it for however many cups of rice you have.

Make the rice however you make your rice.  This isn’t a rice recipe - it’s more of a sauce recipe.

  • 2 Tbsp low sodium soya sauce
  • 2 Tbsp rice vinegar
  • 1.5 Tbsp pineapple juice  (NOTE: don’t be a dope like me and buy pineapple juice.  Just use the juice from the can of pineapple, noted below)
  • 1 Tbsp tomato paste
  • 1 Tbsp brown sugar
  • 1 Tsp corn starch
  • 1 Tbsp water (for corn starch slurry)
  • 1 Medium carrot, julienned
  • 1 Medium red bell pepper, cut thinly
  • 1 Medium green bell pepper, cut thinly
  • 1/2 cup of pineapple chunks (optional, but really, there’s pineapple juice in it for cryin’ out loud.  If you don’t like pineapple, don’t make this)

Instructions

  1. Cook your rice ahead of time however you cook rice.
  2. In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, pineapple juice, tomato paste, and brown sugar to create the sweet and sour sauce. Stir until well combined.
  3. In a separate small bowl, dissolve the cornstarch in 1 tablespoon of water to create a slurry. Set aside.
  4. Heat a large nonstick skillet or wok over medium heat. Add the julienned carrots and sliced bell peppers. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until they are slightly softened but still have some crunch.
  5. Pour the sweet and sour sauce into the skillet with the vegetables. Stir well to coat the vegetables evenly.
  6. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. Gradually add the cornstarch slurry while stirring to thicken the sauce. Allow it to cook for 2-3 minutes until the sauce reaches the desired consistency.
  7. If using, gently fold in the pineapple chunks and cook for an additional minute to warm them through.
  8. Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and serve it onto plates or bowls. Spoon the sweet and sour vegetable mixture over the rice.

Credit for this recipe goes to Snap Calorie:

item.title: Apple Cider Rice Krispie Squares
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2cupunsalted butter, plus more for greasing
  • 2 cupsapple cider or unfiltered apple juice
  • 1 tablespoongranulated sugar
  • 1 teaspoonground cinnamon
  • 210-ounce bags mini marshmallows, divided (about 11 cups total)
  • 1/4teaspoonkosher salt
  • 112-ounce box rice cereal, such as Rice Krispies (about 10 cups)
  • Nonstick cooking spray

Instructions

  1. In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven set over medium heat, melt the butter. Continue cooking, stirring often to prevent the milk solids from burning, until the butter foams and darkens into a light amber color and smells nutty, 4 to 6 minutes. Immediately add the apple cider, increase the heat to high, and bring to a boil. Let the cider boil, stirring occasionally, until it reduces into a thick, bubbling caramel-like consistency that coats the back of a spoon, 18 to 20 minutes.
  2. As the cider is reducing, grease a 9x13-inch glass or metal baking pan. In a small bowl, combine the sugar and cinnamon.
  3. Once the cider has reduced, add one bag of marshmallows and salt and continue cooking over high heat, stirring constantly, until the marshmallows melt and darken into a light caramel color, 3 to 4 minutes.
  4. Remove the pot from the heat and add the remaining bag of marshmallows. Stir just until the marshmallows are about half melted, then add the rice cereal. Gently but quickly mix everything together until the rice cereal is evenly coated in the marshmallow mixture and immediately transfer into the prepared baking pan. Gently press the mixture into an even layer using a spatula sprayed with nonstick cooking spray. Sprinkle the cinnamon sugar mixture over the top and let set for at least 1 hour before cutting.

Credit for this recipe goes to Food52.com:

item.title: Sushi Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups sushi rice
  • 2.5 cups hot water (the hot water will help the Instant Pot come to pressure faster)
  • 2 tbsp rice vinegar
  • 2 tbsp sugar
  • 1 tbsp kosher salt

NOTE: I rinsed my rice ahead of time. This particular recipe didn’t say to do so, but the rice comes out less “gummy” if you do (from what I understand, anyway!). Just put the rice in a big bowl and cover it with cool water. Swish it around with your hand a bit, then pour the now-murky water out. Do this as many times as it takes until the water drains clear.

item.title: Instant Pot White Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup long grain white rice (rinsed or unrinsed)
  • 1 1/4 cup low-sodium chicken broth
  • ½ teaspoon salt
  • ½ tablespoon butter

Instructions

  1. Add rice, chicken broth, salt, and butter to the pressure cooker pot and stir.
  2. Secure the lid and turn pressure release knob to a sealed position. Cook at high pressure for 3 minutes.
  3. When cooking is complete, use a natural release for 10 minutes and then release any remaining pressure.
  4. Fluff the rice with a fork.  Serve immediately.

Credit for this recipe goes to Insta Fresh Meals:

item.title: Instant Pot Rice (Basmati or Jasmine)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Cup Basmati (or Jasmine) rice
  • 1 Cup Water
  • Salt to taste (just a few shakes will do)

Instructions

  1. Put all the ingredients in your Instant Pot.  I give them a little stir for good measure.
  2. Set the IP for 6 mins and go chillax for a bit.
  3. Once those 6 mins are up, let it natural release for 10 mins.
  4. Likely good to get it out of the pot right when it’s done, as it tends to stick to the pot if you let it sit for a bit.

Cleanup is easy.  Just let your pot soak with water for 10 mins and it rinses right out.  Really good for butter chicken, stir fry, etc…  Comes out perfect.

item.title: Chicken (from frozen), Rice, and Carrots
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 and 1/4 cups long grain white rice
  • 2 cups unsalted chicken broth
  • 1 tsp. salt
  • 1/2 tsp. pepper
  • 4 (6-8 ounces each) individually-frozen, boneless, skinless chicken breasts
  • 2 tsp. Italian herb blend, or your favorite dried herb blend
  • 5 -6 large carrots, peeled and cut into 1” pieces

Instructions

  1. Measure rice into bottom of the Instant Pot.
  2. Add chicken broth and salt and pepper, stir to combine.
  3. Lay the frozen chicken breasts on top of the rice. Season the chicken breasts with the Italian herb blend or your favorite herb blend.
  4. Lay carrots on top of the rice.
  5. Cover pot, lock on the lid, and set the steam release valve toSealing
  6. SelectPressure Cookand set to 10 minutes.
  7. When the cooking cycle has finished, allow the pot to sit for 5 minutes, and then do a quick release by moving the valve toVenting.

NOTES

You can make this recipe with fresh chicken breasts as well. Set the cooking time to 7 minutes. Allow the pot to sit for 5 minutes, and then do a quick release by moving the valve toVenting.

item.title: Chicken Broccoli and Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 Tbsp Butter
  • 1½ - 2 Pounds Boneless Skinless Chicken Breast cut into cubes
  • 2 Cloves Garlic
  • 1 Small Onion
  • 1 Tsp Salt
  • 3/4 Tsp Pepper
  • 1 Tsp Garlic Powder
  • 1 and 1/3 Cups Long Grain Rice
  • 1 and 1/3 Cups Chicken Broth
  • ½ Cup Milk
  • 1½ Tbsp Flour
  • 1-2 Cups Fresh Broccoli cooked
  • 1 and 1/2 - 2 Cups Mild Shredded Cheddar Cheese

Instructions

Instructions

  1. Turn Instant Pot onto saute. Add butter and heat until hot. When hot, add chicken, onion, and garlic.
  2. Cook chicken mixture until onion starts to get translucent. Add rice, broth and seasonings. Stir well.
  3. Whisk together milk and flour and set aside.
  4. Cook on manual high pressure for 5 minutes. When the timer is up perform a quick release.
  5. Immediately add milk and flour mixture and mix until it is well combined.
  6. Add broccoli and cheese and stir until well combined. Serve immediately.

Here is a link to the actual website.  Credit to I Don’t Have Time for That:

item.title: Jollof Rice (Yan's)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 bell peppers (ideally red, but I had 1 green and 1 red)
  • 3 roma tomatoes
  • 2 red onions (barf)
  • 2 scotch bonnet peppers
  • 3 garlic cloves
  • 1 tsp fresh ginger
  • Half a cup water
  • ⅔ cup vegetable oil
  • 1 can tomato paste
  • 1 tbsp curry powder
  • 2 tsp dried thyme
  • 3 chicken bouillon cubes
  • Bay leaves
  • 3 cups parboiled long grain rice
  • Salt and pepper to taste (be generous)

Instructions

  1. Roughly chop up bell peppers, tomatoes, 1 of the onions, scotch bonnet peppers, garlic and ginger. Throw that into a blender, add half a cup of water, and get it all liquidy.
